title: Formatting with AS
description: Rename your columns to make them readable.

slides.title: Ugly Column Names 👹
slides.content:
# Making Data Presentable

Often, the columns in a database have ugly, technical names like `usr_frst_nm` or `tot_amt_usd`.

If you present that to your boss or users, they'll be confused!

slides.title: Creating Aliases with AS 🎭
slides.content:
## Give it a nickname

You can temporarily rename a column in your results using the word **`AS`** (called an Alias).

`SELECT usr_frst_nm AS "First Name" FROM users;`

Now, the result grid will beautifully display "First Name" instead of the ugly tech name.

quizData.question: What is the purpose of the AS keyword in SQL?

options.text: To permanently change a column's name in the database
To give a column a temporary, readable nickname in the search results
To tell the database what format to save the file AS

quizData.explanation: AS simply creates an alias (nickname) for the column just for that specific query's output. It does not permanently alter the database structure.

slides.title: Quotes Matter ✍️
slides.content: If your alias has a space in it (like `"First Name"`), you **MUST** put it inside quotes. If there is no space (like `FirstName`), quotes are optional.

quizData.question: Which of the following queries correctly renames the `dob` column to `Date of Birth`?

options.text: SELECT dob AS Date of Birth FROM users;
SELECT dob AS "Date of Birth" FROM users;
SELECT "Date of Birth" AS dob FROM users;

quizData.explanation: Because 'Date of Birth' contains spaces, it must be surrounded by quotations when used as an alias.

slides.title: Try it Out!
slides.content: Retrieve the `max_spd` column from the `cars` table and name it `Top Speed`.
slides.codeSnippet:
SELECT max_spd AS "Top Speed"
FROM cars;
